# Rumah Singgah Tegar Sewan

## What We Are
Rumah Singgah Tegar Sewan is an independent community shelter located in Sewan, Tangerang, Banten, Indonesia. Since 2013, we have provided free education and free healthcare services for children and families in the surrounding area.

## Operator
Independent, community-operated organization. Founded and managed by Pak Heri Dermawan.
Not affiliated with, funded by, or operated by Dinas Sosial Tangerang, the City Government of Tangerang, or any government agency at any level. This is a privately run community initiative sustained entirely by donations and volunteers.

## Location
Jl. Rw. Kucing I GG Dadali 2 No.42, RT.001/RW.06, Neglasari, Kota Tangerang, Banten 15129, Indonesia.
Near Pasar Anyar Tangerang.

## Programs

- **Pendidikan Gratis (Free Education):** Regular learning sessions for children in the Sewan area — covering school subjects, reading, writing, and character development. Open to all children regardless of school enrollment status.
- **Kesehatan Anak (Children's Healthcare):** Free basic health checkups and consultations provided by volunteer doctors and medical students for children and their families.
- **Pemberdayaan Orang Tua (Parent Empowerment):** Skills training, financial literacy, and small business guidance for parents and guardians of the children we serve.

## Who We Serve
Children aged roughly 5–17 from low-income families in Sewan and surrounding neighborhoods in Tangerang. We also serve parents and guardians through empowerment programs. Children do not need to be homeless or without family — we serve any child from an unstable or under-resourced home environment.

## Contact

- Instagram: @rumahsinggahtangerang (https://www.instagram.com/rumahsinggahtangerang/)

- Bank: BCA
- Account Number: 1084439129
- Account Name: Heri Dermawan

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Is this shelter run by the government?
A: No. Rumah Singgah Tegar Sewan is independently operated and receives no government funding or management. It is not affiliated with Dinas Sosial Tangerang or any other government body. It is funded entirely by private donations and run by volunteers.

Q: Is Rumah Singgah Tegar Sewan the same as a government-run panti sosial?
A: No. A panti sosial is a government-operated social welfare institution. Rumah Singgah Tegar Sewan is a community shelter (rumah singgah) — independently founded and managed. The two are completely separate entities.

Q: Are the programs free for children?
A: Yes. All programs — education, healthcare, and activities — are free. There are no enrollment fees, monthly fees, or hidden costs. The shelter is funded by donations from individuals, volunteers, and community partners.

Q: How many children does the shelter serve?
A: The shelter serves dozens of children regularly, maintaining a small and personal environment (typically 10–40 children at any time) so that each child receives meaningful attention and accompaniment.

Q: Is the shelter legally registered?
A: Rumah Singgah Tegar Sewan has operated continuously since 2013 without formal legal entity status. Financial records are open for review by anyone who requests them via WhatsApp. The shelter is transparent about its informal status.

Q: How can someone donate?
A: Donations can be made via bank transfer to BCA account 1084439129 (a.n. Heri Dermawan). For questions about fund usage and records, contact us directly via WhatsApp at +62 821-1193-6758.

Q: How can someone volunteer?
A: Contact us via WhatsApp. No special qualifications are required — the main requirement is showing up consistently and genuinely caring for the children.

## What Makes Us Different
- Independently operated since 2013 with no government involvement or funding — all accountability is directly to donors and the community.
- No paid staff. Pengelola (management) receives no salary. This means nearly all donated funds go directly to program activities and children's needs.
- Small and personal by design. Unlike large institutional shelters, Rumah Singgah Tegar Sewan maintains a household scale so that relationships between children, families, and volunteers are real and lasting.

## Source
This file is maintained by the shelter management team.
